name: Blackpink
description:
'''Blackpink''' ({{korean|hangul=블랙핑크}}; stylized in [[all caps]]) is a South Korean [[girl group]] formed by [[YG Entertainment]]. The group is composed of four members: [[Jisoo]], [[Jennie (singer)|Jennie]], [[Rosé (singer)|Rosé]], and [[Lisa (rapper)|Lisa]]. Regarded by various publications as the "biggest girl group in the world", they are recognized as a leading force in the [[Korean Wave]] and an ambassador of the "girl crush" concept in [[K-pop]], which explores themes of [[Confidence|self-confidence]] and [[Women's empowerment|female empowerment]].

Blackpink debuted in August 2016 with their [[Single (music)#In South Korea|single album]] ''[[Square One (single album)|Square One]]'', which included "[[Whistle (Blackpink song)|Whistle]]", their first number-one hit on South Korea's [[Circle Digital Chart]], and "[[Boombayah]]", their first number-one on the US ''[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]'' [[World Digital Song Sales|World Digital Songs]] chart. The group achieved an international breakthrough with "[[Ddu-Du Ddu-Du]]" (2018), which topped South Korean charts and marked the first song by a Korean female group to enter the [[UK singles chart|UK Singles Chart]] and receive a [[RIAA certification|certification]] from the [[Recording Industry Association of America]] (RIAA). Its music video was the first by a Korean group to surpass one and two billion views on [[YouTube]]. Their music videos for "[[Kill This Love (song)|Kill This Love]]" (2019) and "[[How You Like That]]" (2020) set records for the most-viewed videos within the first 24 hours of release; the latter topped the Circle Digital Chart and set multiple [[Guinness World Records]].

Blackpink's debut studio album, ''[[The Album (Blackpink album)|The Album]]'' (2020), was the first [[List of best-selling albums in South Korea|album]] by a Korean female act to sell one million copies. Their 2022 follow-up, ''[[Born Pink]],'' was the first to sell two million copies and the first by a Korean girl group to reach number one on the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]] and the [[UK Albums Chart]]. The album's lead single "[[Pink Venom]]" was the first song by a Korean group to top Australia's [[ARIA Charts|ARIA Singles Chart]] and the first by a girl group to top the [[Billboard Global 200|''Billboard'' Global 200]]. Blackpink earned further global number-one singles with "[[Shut Down (Blackpink song)|Shut Down]]" (2022) and "[[Jump (Blackpink song)|Jump]]" (2025) and has the most US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] entries among Korean female acts. Their [[Born Pink World Tour]] (2022–23) was the [[List of highest-grossing concert tours by women|highest-grossing concert tour by a female group]] and Asian act in history, while they became the first Asian act to headline [[Coachella]] in 2023. Their extended play ''[[Deadline (EP)|Deadline]]'' (2026) set first-day and first-week sales records for a Korean girl group.

With over 40 billion streams and 20 million records sold worldwide, Blackpink is one of the [[List of best-selling girl groups|best-selling girl groups of all time]]. They are the [[List of most-subscribed YouTube channels|most-subscribed]] music act on YouTube and the first to surpass 100 million subscribers, as well as the most-viewed band on the platform. They are also the [[List of most-streamed artists on Spotify#Most-followed artists|most-followed]] and most-streamed female group on [[Spotify]]. Blackpink's [[List of awards and nominations received by Blackpink|accolades]] include a [[Billboard Music Award|''Billboard'' Music Award]], an [[MTV Europe Music Award]], and multiple [[Golden Disc Awards]] and [[MTV Video Music Awards]]; they were the first girl group to win [[MTV Video Music Award for Best Group|Group of the Year]] at the latter awards in the 21st century. Outside of music, they have endorsement deals in various industries and incorporate fashion into their public image. For their work as advocates for [[climate change]] awareness, the group was invested as honorary [[Order of the British Empire|Members of the Order of the British Empire]] (MBE) in 2023. Blackpink was the first girl group to enter ''Forbes''{{'}} [[Forbes 30 Under 30|30 Under 30 Asia]] and was named ''[[Time (magazine)|Time]]''{{'}}s 2022 [[Time Person of the Year#Other categories|Entertainer of the Year]]. The group has been ranked among the top of the [[Forbes Korea Power Celebrity 40|''Forbes'' Korea Power Celebrity 40]] list and recognized by former South Korean president [[Moon Jae-in]] for spreading K-pop and Korean culture worldwide.
